title: Fatal Accident

statuteRef.label: Special time limit for actions under Fatal Accidents Act 1976

questions.label: Date of death
questions.helpText: The date on which the deceased died. Under s.12(2) Limitation Act 1980, the 3-year period runs from the date of death or the date of knowledge of the dependant bringing the claim, whichever is later.

questions.label: Do you know the dependant's date of knowledge?
questions.helpText: Under s.12(2), the date of knowledge for a fatal accident claim is when the dependant first knew: (a) the injury that caused death was attributable to the defendant's act or omission, (b) the defendant's identity, and (c) supporting facts. If the dependant learned of these facts after the death, the 3-year period may run from this later date.

questions.label: Date of knowledge of the dependant
questions.helpText: The date the dependant bringing the claim first had the relevant knowledge described above.

questions.label: Was the dependant under a disability at the date of death?
questions.helpText: Under the Limitation Act 1980, a person is under a disability if they are a minor (under 18) or lack capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005.

questions.label: Date disability ceased
questions.helpText: The date the dependant turned 18, or the date capacity was regained.

notes: A fatal accident claim can only be brought if the deceased would have had a valid claim had they survived (s.1(1) Fatal Accidents Act 1976).
If the deceased's own claim was already time-barred before death, the dependants' fatal accident claim is also barred.
The court has discretion under s.33 Limitation Act 1980 to disapply the time limit.
A separate claim for the estate under the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1934 may have different limitation considerations.
